easy.prompt: Which query returns ALL columns and rows from the students table?
answers: SELECT name FROM students;
SELECT * FROM students;
GET ALL FROM students;
SHOW students.*;

easy.prompt: Which query returns only the name and grade columns from students?
answers: SELECT name, grade FROM students;
SELECT students(name, grade);
GET name, grade FROM students;
SELECT * WHERE name, grade FROM students;

easy.prompt: How do you get all employees who work in the Engineering department?
answers: SELECT * FROM employees HAVING department = 'Engineering';
SELECT department = 'Engineering' FROM employees;
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department = 'Engineering';
FILTER employees WHERE department = 'Engineering';

easy.prompt: Which query returns all products with a price greater than 1000?
answers: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 1000;
SELECT * FROM products IF price > 1000;
SELECT price > 1000 FROM products;
SELECT * FROM products HAVING price > 1000;

easy.prompt: How do you get the 5 cheapest books ordered by price ascending?
answers: SELECT * FROM books FIRST 5 ORDER price;
SELECT TOP 5 * FROM books ORDER BY price;
SELECT * FROM books ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 5;
SELECT * FROM books LIMIT 5;

easy.prompt: Which query returns all students who live in Cairo?
answers: SELECT * FROM students HAVING city = 'Cairo';
SELECT city = 'Cairo' FROM students;
SELECT * FROM students WHERE city IS 'Cairo';
SELECT * FROM students WHERE city = 'Cairo';

easy.prompt: Which query returns all movies with a rating above 7.5?
answers: SELECT * FROM movies HAVING rating > 7.5;
SELECT * FROM movies WHERE rating > 7.5;
SELECT * FROM movies IF rating > 7.5;
SELECT rating > 7.5 FROM movies;

easy.prompt: How do you sort all products by price from highest to lowest?
answers: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price;
SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price ASC;
SELECT * FROM products SORT price DESC;
SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;

easy.prompt: Which query returns employees whose salary is less than 6000?
answers: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary < 6000;
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > 6000;
SELECT salary < 6000 FROM employees;
SELECT * FROM employees HAVING salary < 6000;

easy.prompt: How do you get all orders with status equal to 'pending'?
answers: SELECT * FROM orders IF status = 'pending';
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status LIKE 'pending';
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';
SELECT status = 'pending' FROM orders;

easy.prompt: Which query returns only the name and city columns from customers?
answers: SELECT customers.name, city;
SELECT name, city FROM customers;
GET name, city IN customers;
SELECT name AND city FROM customers;

easy.prompt: Which query returns all movies released in the year 2022?
answers: SELECT * FROM movies WHERE year IS 2022;
SELECT * FROM movies HAVING year = 2022;
SELECT * FROM movies WHERE year = 2022;
SELECT * FROM movies IF year == 2022;

easy.prompt: Which JOIN keeps all rows from the customers table, even if they have no matching orders?
answers: SELECT * FROM orders LEFT JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id;
SELECT * FROM customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id;
SELECT * FROM customers LEFT JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id;
SELECT * FROM customers RIGHT JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id;

easy.prompt: Which query returns all rows from both tables, filling NULLs where there is no match?
answers: SELECT * FROM employees INNER JOIN departments ON employees.dept_id = departments.id;
SELECT * FROM employees FULL OUTER JOIN departments ON employees.dept_id = departments.id;
SELECT * FROM employees LEFT JOIN departments ON employees.dept_id = departments.id;
SELECT * FROM employees CROSS JOIN departments ON employees.dept_id = departments.id;

easy.prompt: Which join produces every possible combination of rows between two tables (no ON condition needed)?
answers: SELECT * FROM colors INNER JOIN sizes ON colors.id = sizes.id;
SELECT * FROM colors FULL OUTER JOIN sizes;
SELECT * FROM colors LEFT JOIN sizes ON colors.id = sizes.id;
SELECT * FROM colors CROSS JOIN sizes;

medium.prompt: Which query counts the total number of rows in the orders table?
answers: SELECT TOTAL(*) FROM orders;
SELECT SUM(*) FROM orders;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders;
SELECT NUMBER(*) FROM orders;

medium.prompt: Which query shows the total salary paid per department?
answers: SELECT department, SUM(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY department;
SELECT department, SUM(salary) FROM employees;
SELECT SUM(salary) FROM employees GROUP department;
SELECT department, TOTAL(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY department;

medium.prompt: Which query finds the average product price in each category?
answers: SELECT category, AVG(price) FROM products;
SELECT category, AVG(price) FROM products GROUP BY category;
SELECT AVG(price) FROM products GROUP category;
SELECT category, AVERAGE(price) FROM products GROUP BY category;

medium.prompt: Which query joins orders with customers to show customer name and order amount?
answers: SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c, orders o WHERE id = customer_id;
SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id;
SELECT name, amount FROM customers JOIN orders;
SELECT c.name, o.amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.id;

medium.prompt: Which query shows distinct customer names who placed at least one order?
answers: SELECT c.name FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id;
SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id;
SELECT UNIQUE c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id;
SELECT c.name FROM customers c WHERE orders EXISTS;

medium.prompt: Which query combines the names of all customers and all suppliers into one result, removing duplicates?
answers: SELECT name FROM customers JOIN suppliers ON customers.id = suppliers.id;
SELECT name FROM customers UNION ALL SELECT name FROM suppliers;
SELECT name FROM customers UNION SELECT name FROM suppliers;
SELECT name FROM customers, suppliers;

medium.prompt: Which query shows departments with more than 2 employees?
answers: SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM employees WHERE COUNT(*) > 2 GROUP BY department;
SELECT department FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;
SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;
SELECT department FROM employees HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;

medium.prompt: Which query returns the maximum salary from the employees table?
answers: SELECT HIGHEST(salary) FROM employees;
SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees;
SELECT salary ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 1 FROM employees;
SELECT TOP(salary) FROM employees;

medium.prompt: Which query shows the minimum price of products in each category?
answers: SELECT category, MIN(price) FROM products;
SELECT MIN(price) FROM products GROUP category;
SELECT category, MIN(price) FROM products GROUP BY category;
SELECT category, LOWEST(price) FROM products GROUP BY category;

medium.prompt: Which query counts how many products are in each category?
answers: SELECT category, COUNT(*) FROM products;
SELECT category, COUNT(*) FROM products GROUP BY category;
SELECT COUNT(category) FROM products GROUP BY category;
SELECT category, SUM(1) FROM products;

medium.prompt: Which query joins employees with departments to show employee name and department budget?
answers: SELECT e.name, d.budget FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON e.department = d.name;
SELECT e.name, d.budget FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.id = d.id;
SELECT e.name, d.budget FROM employees e, departments d;
SELECT name, budget FROM employees JOIN departments ON id;

medium.prompt: Which query shows each customer's name and their total spending across all orders?
answers: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.amount) FROM customers c JOIN orders o;
SELECT c.name, SUM(o.amount) AS total FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name;
SELECT c.name, SUM(o.amount) FROM customers c JOIN orders o GROUP BY o.amount;
SELECT name, SUM(amount) FROM customers, orders GROUP BY name;

medium.prompt: Which JOIN returns all employees and their department name, including employees with no department assigned?
answers: SELECT e.name, d.name FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;
SELECT e.name, d.name FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON e.dept_id = d.id;
SELECT e.name, d.name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;
SELECT e.name, d.name FROM employees e RIGHT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;

medium.prompt: You want to find employees who belong to NO department AND departments that have NO employees. Which query achieves this using a FULL OUTER JOIN?
answers: SELECT * FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id WHERE d.id IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id WHERE e.dept_id IS NULL OR d.id IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM employees e FULL OUTER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id WHERE e.dept_id IS NULL OR d.id IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM employees e FULL OUTER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id WHERE e.dept_id IS NULL AND d.id IS NULL;

medium.prompt: Which query uses a CROSS JOIN to combine every color with every size, producing all possible pairs?
answers: SELECT c.name, s.label FROM colors c CROSS JOIN sizes s;
SELECT c.name, s.label FROM colors c INNER JOIN sizes s ON c.id = s.id;
SELECT c.name, s.label FROM colors c LEFT JOIN sizes s ON c.id = s.color_id;
SELECT c.name, s.label FROM colors c FULL OUTER JOIN sizes s ON c.id = s.id;

hard.prompt: Which query finds products that cost MORE than the average product price?
answers: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > AVG(price);
SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > (SELECT AVG(price) FROM products);
SELECT * FROM products HAVING price > AVG(price);
SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > (SELECT price FROM products);
A join where a table is joined to itself using aliases.
A join that returns only unmatched rows from two different tables.
A join that automatically removes duplicate rows from a table.
A join that can only be used with three or more tables.

hard.prompt: SQLite used to not support FULL OUTER JOIN directly. What was the common workaround?
answers: Use only INNER JOIN and add DISTINCT.
Combine two LEFT JOIN queries with UNION.
Use CROSS JOIN with a WHERE filter.
Use GROUP BY on both tables without joins.

hard.prompt: The students table has 30 rows. The grades table has entries for only 20 of those students. How many rows will INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN each return?
answers: INNER JOIN = 30, LEFT JOIN = 20
INNER JOIN = 20, LEFT JOIN = 30
INNER JOIN = 30, LEFT JOIN = 30
INNER JOIN = 20, LEFT JOIN = 20

hard.prompt: SQLite used to  not support FULL OUTER JOIN. Which query correctly simulates it using UNION?
answers: SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON a.id = b.id UNION ALL SELECT * FROM a RIGHT JOIN b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON a.id = b.id UNION SELECT * FROM b LEFT JOIN a ON b.id = a.id;
SELECT * FROM a INNER JOIN b ON a.id = b.id UNION SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT * FROM a CROSS JOIN b UNION SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON a.id = b.id;

hard.prompt: When is CASE WHEN in a SELECT statement most useful?
answers: When you need to join two tables by primary key.
When you need to sort rows in descending order.
When you want conditional labels or values in the result, like status groups.
When you want to remove all duplicate rows from the table permanently.

hard.prompt: Which query finds the SECOND highest salary in the employees table?
answers: SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
SELECT salary FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees WHERE salary < (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees);
SELECT SECOND(salary) FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC;
It sorts rows alphabetically by default.
It returns unique combinations of the selected columns, removing duplicate result rows.
It removes duplicate rows from the database table itself.
It only works when selecting a single numeric column.

hard.prompt: Which query finds departments where the MINIMUM salary of all employees is above 4500?
answers: SELECT department FROM employees WHERE salary > 4500 GROUP BY department;
SELECT department FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING MIN(salary) > 4500;
SELECT department FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING ALL salary > 4500;
SELECT department FROM employees WHERE MIN(salary) > 4500;

hard.prompt: What is the key difference between UNION and UNION ALL?
answers: UNION ALL is always slower than UNION because it does extra work.
UNION ALL removes duplicates; UNION keeps all rows including duplicates.
UNION removes duplicate rows from the combined result; UNION ALL keeps all rows including duplicates.
UNION requires both queries to return the same number of rows; UNION ALL does not.

hard.prompt: Why are table aliases (like e, d, c) useful in SQL queries?
answers: They change the table name permanently in the database.
They force SQLite to use indexes for all queries.
They make queries shorter and clearer, especially when joining multiple tables or self joining.
They automatically remove NULL values from result rows.

hard.prompt: What is the main difference between WHERE and HAVING?
answers: WHERE filters grouped results, while HAVING filters rows before grouping.
WHERE and HAVING are identical and always interchangeable.
WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY.
HAVING can be used only with ORDER BY, not with GROUP BY.

hard.prompt: Table colors has 4 rows. Table sizes has 6 rows. How many rows does SELECT * FROM colors CROSS JOIN sizes return?
What is an anti-join pattern used for in SQL?
answers: To return only rows that match in both tables.
To create every possible row combination between two tables.
To find rows in one table that do not have a match in another table.
To update rows in both tables at the same time.

hard.prompt: You want all rows from table A that have NO matching row in table B (anti-join). Which query correctly achieves this?
answers: SELECT * FROM a INNER JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id WHERE b.a_id IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id WHERE b.a_id IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM a FULL OUTER JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id WHERE a.id IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM a CROSS JOIN b WHERE b.a_id IS NULL;
